mechanics.text:
GAME CONCEPTS
After the Dice Roll character can wait and see what the dice say before spending Edge However; each point spent after the dice roll only increases or decreases the result by one. Alternatively, the character can spend one Edge point to completely reroll the die result: While this might seem like bet- ter option_ the second result stands even if it is worse than the first, and Edge cannot be spent on the second die roll at all: However; this use  of Edge   does give you useful second chance your roll was way off the mark: The use of Edge to affect die roll already made must be announced and resolved immediately after the die roll made_ Once ay other die rolls have been made Edge can no longer be used.
RECOVERING YOUR EDGE When character pushes his luck; the Edge spent is tem porarily deducted from his Edge Attribute This affects him as any reduced Attribute would until the spent Edge is recovered_ The reduction is not permanent, SO you need not recover the ost Edge through actual character advancement: The char acter' maximum Edge Attribute   represents the character $ average helping" of good fortune. His current Edge represents reduction in fortune due t0 pushing his luck. This means that recovering the normal level of Edge requires sort of balanc ing of the scales fortune wise_ This can be accomplished in three ways_ as described below:
The Golden Rule As they say; what goes around comes around The best way to recover from that extra dose of luck you got I5 t0 give luck to other people in the form of good deeds  As such your Edge   will bounce back whenever you something selfless or generous. Note that these acts need not be spectacular (saving planet from destruction) but on the other hand cannot be trivial (helping an aged ComStar Precentor cross the street). Ultimately; to the gamemaster to decide what deeds are worthy of Edge recovery and how many points are recovered per deed The scale of deeds should also be balanced against the character and his station and means For example donating large sum of money to charity might qualify; but what consti: tutes "large sum' varies from one character t0 another:
example is when the die roll for an important action results in an abysmal failure (especially fumble) . causing the character embarrassment or even injury: Although the failure will hurt the character in the short term; he will at least regain some lost Edgel
Live and Learn The most mechanical" way t0 recover Edge but the only method open t0 characters who are both amoral and lucky; I5 t0 simply spend accumulated Experience Points (XPI This repre sents character learning from his mistakes ad applying that knowledge general competence rather than specific skill. This   competence and   preparedness come across the untrained eye as luck, and the game it is represented by Edge . Rules gaining and spending can found Character Advancement; starting on 209.
Karmlc Backlash When something horrible happens t0 your character; there an up side: You will regain lost Edge points Unlike the Golden Rule method , regaining Edge in this manner is never intention: represents the balance that occurs when the cosmic dice are weighted against the character; each  stroke of bad luck evens out the books even as it sets the character back: The gamemaster must determine when something iS horri: ble or unlucky enough to warrant Edge recovery. common
